meta.title: Farm & Property Details
meta.topic: Where each piece of information belongs. Farm = the business; a farm can have several properties; each property can have several paddocks (the field a trial runs on).

questions.text: Which country are you answering for?
questions.help: So we can compare answers across countries. If you cover more than one, pick 'Head office / multiple' and add detail in the note.

questions.text: Is a farm always located at one of its properties, or can its address be somewhere else (an office or home in town)?
options: Always at one of its properties

questions.text: When you add a new farm, does it always already have a property, or can a farm be on your books before any property is pinned down?
Are contacts held at the farm level, the property level, or both?
questions.help: A farm can have several contacts either way — this is just about where they sit.

questions.text: For each contact, what do you need to know?
Main crops — recorded for each property, summarised for the whole farm, or both?
Farming experience — recorded about the farm overall, or does it differ by property?
Do you need a single total farming-area figure for the whole farm, or is the size of each property enough (they add up)?
Is the land-size unit (hectares or acres) fixed for the whole farm, or chosen per property?
questions.help: Feel free to note which unit you normally use.
options: Same unit for the whole farm

questions.text: How much a farm can commit to a trial, or why they want to trial — is that about the farm in general, or decided fresh for each trial?
Would those preferences ever differ between a farm's properties?
